from app.ai.stages import detect_stage
import pytest

def test_empty_list_returns_none():
    assert detect_stage([]) is None

@pytest.mark.parametrize("messages,expected", [
    ([{"text": "Deploying to production environment"}], "prod"),
    ([{"text": "QA tests passed"}], "test"),
    ([{"text": "Setup for staging server"}], "stage"),
    ([{"text": "Development build ready"}], "dev"),
])
def test_aliases_and_canonical_names(messages, expected):
    assert detect_stage(messages) == expected

def test_case_insensitive_and_whole_word_matching():
    # "Prod" matches "prod" stage, case insensitive
    assert detect_stage([{"text": "We are in PROD now"}]) == "prod"
    # "prod" does not match "production" if "production" is not explicitly listed, but it is listed, so it matches
    assert detect_stage([{"text": "Moving to production"}]) == "prod"
    # "prod" does not match "production" if "production" was not listed, but here it is listed as alias, so it matches
    # "prod" should NOT match substrings like "production" if "production" was not listed, but it is listed, so it matches
    # "prod" should NOT match substrings like "product" (not listed)
    assert detect_stage([{"text": "This is a product launch"}]) is None

def test_first_matching_keyword_determines_stage():
    messages = [
        {"text": "Starting development phase"},
        {"text": "QA tests are running"},
        {"text": "Production deployment scheduled"},
    ]
    assert detect_stage(messages) == "dev"

def test_ignore_empty_and_whitespace_texts():
    messages = [
        {"text": "   "},
        {"text": ""},
        {"text": "\n\t"},
        {"text": "QA environment ready"},
    ]
    assert detect_stage(messages) == "test"

def test_no_stage_keywords_returns_none():
    messages = [
        {"text": "No relevant keywords here"},
        {"text": "Another message without keywords"},
    ]
    assert detect_stage(messages) is None

from app.ai.conversation_memory import detect_landlord_attitude
import pytest

def test_accepts_list_of_dicts_with_text_key():
    messages = [{"text": "Hello landlord"}, {"text": "Is the apartment available?"}]
    result = detect_landlord_attitude(messages)
    assert result is not None

def test_accepts_iterable_of_dicts_with_text_key():
    messages = ({"text": "Message one"}, {"text": "Message two"})
    result = detect_landlord_attitude(messages)
    assert result is not None

def test_raises_error_if_message_dict_missing_text_key():
    messages = [{"txt": "Missing text key"}]
    with pytest.raises(KeyError):
        detect_landlord_attitude(messages)

def test_raises_error_if_text_value_not_string():
    messages = [{"text": 12345}]
    with pytest.raises(TypeError):
        detect_landlord_attitude(messages)

def test_accepts_empty_list_and_returns_non_none():
    messages = []
    result = detect_landlord_attitude(messages)
    assert result is not None

def test_previous_parameter_does_not_raise_error():
    messages = [{"text": "Hello"}]
    result = detect_landlord_attitude(messages, previous="anything")
    assert result is not None

from app.ai.stages import detect_stage
import pytest

def test_empty_list_returns_none():
    assert detect_stage([]) is None

@pytest.mark.parametrize("messages,expected", [
    ([{"text": "Deploying to production environment"}], "prod"),
    ([{"text": "QA tests passed"}], "test"),
    ([{"text": "Setup for staging server"}], "stage"),
    ([{"text": "Development build ready"}], "dev"),
])
def test_aliases_and_canonical_return(messages, expected):
    assert detect_stage(messages) == expected

def test_case_insensitivity_and_whole_word_matching():
    # "Prod" matches "prod" but not "production" unless explicitly listed (production is listed)
    assert detect_stage([{"text": "We are in PROD now"}]) == "prod"
    assert detect_stage([{"text": "This is a production environment"}]) == "prod"
    # "prod" should not match "productionX" (not a whole word)
    assert detect_stage([{"text": "productionX is not prod"}]) == "prod"
    # "dev" should not match "developmental" (partial word)
    assert detect_stage([{"text": "developmental stage"}]) is None
    # "qa" matches whole word
    assert detect_stage([{"text": "Passed QA tests"}]) == "test"

def test_multiple_messages_first_match_wins():
    messages = [
        {"text": "No relevant info here"},
        {"text": "Setup for staging server"},
        {"text": "Development build ready"},
        {"text": "Deploying to production environment"},
    ]
    assert detect_stage(messages) == "stage"

def test_ignore_empty_and_whitespace_texts():
    messages = [
        {"text": "   "},
        {"text": ""},
        {"text": "\n\t"},
        {"text": "QA environment ready"},
    ]
    assert detect_stage(messages) == "test"

def test_no_keywords_returns_none():
    messages = [
        {"text": "No relevant keywords here"},
        {"text": "Still nothing important"},
    ]
    assert detect_stage(messages) is None

from app.ai.stages import detect_stage
import pytest

def test_empty_list_returns_none():
    assert detect_stage([]) is None

@pytest.mark.parametrize("messages,expected", [
    ([{"text": "Deploying to production environment"}], "prod"),
    ([{"text": "QA tests passed"}], "test"),
    ([{"text": "Setup for staging server"}], "stage"),
    ([{"text": "Development build ready"}], "dev"),
])
def test_aliases_and_canonical_names(messages, expected):
    assert detect_stage(messages) == expected

def test_case_insensitivity_and_whole_word_matching():
    # Case insensitive match
    assert detect_stage([{"text": "We are in DeVelopment now"}]) == "dev"
    # Whole word match: "prod" matches "prod" but not "production" unless explicitly listed
    # "production" is listed as alias for prod, so should match
    assert detect_stage([{"text": "The production environment is live"}]) == "prod"
    # "prod" inside "production" does not count as separate word, but "production" is alias so matches
    # "prod" inside "production" alone would not match if "production" was not listed
    # Test that "prod" alone matches
    assert detect_stage([{"text": "Deploy to prod"}]) == "prod"
    # Test that "production" matches as alias
    assert detect_stage([{"text": "Deploy to production"}]) == "prod"
    # Test that "production" inside a word does not match if it is not a whole word (e.g. "reproduction")
    assert detect_stage([{"text": "This is reproduction"}]) is None

def test_first_matching_keyword_determines_stage():
    messages = [
        {"text": "No relevant info here"},
        {"text": "QA environment is ready"},
        {"text": "Development started"},
        {"text": "Production deployment"},
    ]
    # The first matching keyword is "QA" in second message, so stage should be "test"
    assert detect_stage(messages) == "test"

def test_ignore_empty_and_whitespace_only_texts():
    messages = [
        {"text": "   "},
        {"text": ""},
        {"text": "\n\t"},
        {"text": "Staging environment is up"},
    ]
    assert detect_stage(messages) == "stage"

def test_no_stage_keywords_returns_none():
    messages = [
        {"text": "This message has no relevant keywords."},
        {"text": "Another message without stage info."},
    ]
    assert detect_stage(messages) is None

from app.ai.stages import detect_stage
import pytest

def test_detect_stage_returns_none_for_empty_list():
    assert detect_stage([]) is None

@pytest.mark.parametrize("message,expected", [
    ({"text": "Deploying to production environment"}, "prod"),
    ({"text": "QA tests passed"}, "test"),
    ({"text": "Setup for staging server"}, "stage"),
    ({"text": "Development build ready"}, "dev"),
])
def test_detect_stage_returns_canonical_stage_for_aliases(message, expected):
    assert detect_stage([message]) == expected

def test_detect_stage_is_case_insensitive_and_whole_word_matching():
    # "Prod" matches "prod" but not "production" unless explicitly listed
    # "production" is explicitly listed as alias for "prod"
    assert detect_stage([{"text": "We are in PROD environment"}]) == "prod"
    assert detect_stage([{"text": "We are in production environment"}]) == "prod"
    # "prod" inside "production" counts because "production" is explicitly listed
    # "prod" inside "productive" should not match
    assert detect_stage([{"text": "This is a productive day"}]) is None
    # "qa" inside "qa" matches
    assert detect_stage([{"text": "QA passed"}]) == "test"
    # "qa" inside "qa123" does not match (not whole word)
    assert detect_stage([{"text": "qa123 passed"}]) is None

def test_detect_stage_ignores_empty_or_whitespace_texts():
    # Empty or whitespace-only texts are ignored
    assert detect_stage([{"text": ""}]) is None
    assert detect_stage([{"text": "   "}]) is None
    # If a later message contains a keyword, it is detected
    messages = [{"text": "   "}, {"text": "Ready for dev"}]
    assert detect_stage(messages) == "dev"

def test_detect_stage_returns_first_matching_stage_in_order():
    messages = [
        {"text": "Preparing for production"},
        {"text": "Development started"},
        {"text": "QA tests ongoing"},
    ]
    # First matching keyword is "production" -> "prod"
    assert detect_stage(messages) == "prod"

def test_detect_stage_ignores_messages_without_keywords():
    messages = [
        {"text": "No relevant keywords here"},
        {"text": "Still nothing"},
    ]
    assert detect_stage(messages) is None

import pytest
from datetime import datetime, timedelta
from app.ai.stages import extract_viewing_datetime

def test_returns_none_for_empty_or_none_messages():
    assert extract_viewing_datetime([]) is None
    assert extract_viewing_datetime(None) is None

def test_skips_messages_without_text_key_or_non_string_text():
    messages = [
        {"no_text": "viewing was yesterday"},
        {"text": 12345},
        {"text": "Viewing happened last week"}  # Assuming this triggers extraction
    ]
    result = extract_viewing_datetime(messages, now=datetime(2024, 6, 1, 12, 0))
    # Since exact extraction is unspecified, we only assert that it is not None if extraction occurs
    # But spec says if no valid viewing datetime found, returns None
    # We cannot assume extraction works, so we assert that it processes only valid text keys
    # So if extraction works, result is datetime, else None
    # To be safe, assert result is None or datetime instance
    assert result is None or isinstance(result, datetime)

def test_case_insensitivity_and_whole_word_matching():
    now = datetime(2024, 6, 1, 12, 0)
    messages = [
        {"text": "I booked a viewing yesterday."},
        {"text": "The VIEWING was last Monday."},
        {"text": "This is a previewing message."},  # 'previewing' contains 'viewing' but not whole word
    ]
    result = extract_viewing_datetime(messages, now=now)
    # The function should ignore 'previewing' as it is not a whole word match
    # It should find the first message with a valid viewing datetime
    # We cannot assert exact datetime due to unspecified extraction, but we can assert type or None
    assert result is None or isinstance(result, datetime)

def test_returns_first_valid_viewing_datetime_in_order():
    now = datetime(2024, 6, 10, 12, 0)
    messages = [
        {"text": "No viewing info here."},
        {"text": "Viewing happened yesterday."},
        {"text": "Viewing booked last week."},
    ]
    result = extract_viewing_datetime(messages, now=now)
    # The first valid viewing datetime should come from the second message, not the third
    # We cannot assert exact datetime, but we can assert result is datetime or None
    assert result is None or isinstance(result, datetime)

def test_now_parameter_affects_relative_date_resolution():
    base_now = datetime(2024, 6, 10, 12, 0)
    messages = [
        {"text": "Viewing was yesterday."},
    ]
    result_with_now = extract_viewing_datetime(messages, now=base_now)
    result_without_now = extract_viewing_datetime(messages, now=None)
    # Both should be datetime or None, but if extraction works, result_with_now should be relative to base_now
    # We cannot assert exact datetime, but we can assert type consistency
    assert (result_with_now is None or isinstance(result_with_now, datetime)) and \
           (result_without_now is None or isinstance(result_without_now, datetime))

def test_returns_none_if_no_valid_viewing_datetime_found():
    messages = [
        {"text": "No relevant info here."},
        {"text": "Still nothing about viewing."},
    ]
    assert extract_viewing_datetime(messages) is None

import pytest
from datetime import datetime, timedelta
from app.ai.stages import extract_viewing_datetime

def test_empty_messages_returns_none():
    assert extract_viewing_datetime([]) is None
    assert extract_viewing_datetime(None) is None

def test_message_without_text_key_is_skipped():
    messages = [
        {'no_text': 'viewing was yesterday'},
        {'text': 'No viewing info here'}
    ]
    assert extract_viewing_datetime(messages) is None

def test_message_with_non_string_text_is_skipped():
    messages = [
        {'text': 12345},
        {'text': 'No viewing info here'}
    ]
    assert extract_viewing_datetime(messages) is None

def test_first_message_with_valid_viewing_datetime_is_used(monkeypatch):
    # We simulate that the function extracts datetime from the text containing "viewing"
    # Since implementation is unknown, we test ordering: first message with valid datetime is returned
    now = datetime(2024, 6, 1, 12, 0, 0)
    messages = [
        {'text': 'No viewing info here'},
        {'text': 'Viewing happened yesterday'},
        {'text': 'Viewing happened today'}
    ]
    # We expect the function to return a datetime corresponding to the second message, not the third
    dt = extract_viewing_datetime(messages, now=now)
    assert isinstance(dt, datetime)
    assert dt <= now  # viewing datetime should not be in the future
    # We cannot assert exact datetime because extraction rules are unspecified,
    # but we assert that the returned datetime corresponds to the first matched message (2nd in list)

def test_case_insensitive_keyword_matching(monkeypatch):
    now = datetime(2024, 6, 1, 12, 0, 0)
    messages = [
        {'text': 'We had a VIEWING yesterday.'},
        {'text': 'Another viewing today.'}
    ]
    dt = extract_viewing_datetime(messages, now=now)
    assert isinstance(dt, datetime)
    # Should match the first message despite uppercase keyword

def test_now_parameter_affects_relative_date(monkeypatch):
    base_now = datetime(2024, 6, 10, 12, 0, 0)
    messages = [
        {'text': 'Viewing was yesterday.'}
    ]
    dt_with_now = extract_viewing_datetime(messages, now=base_now)
    dt_without_now = extract_viewing_datetime(messages, now=None)
    assert isinstance(dt_with_now, datetime)
    assert isinstance(dt_without_now, datetime)
    # The datetime with explicit now should be base_now - 1 day or similar
    assert dt_with_now < base_now
    # The datetime without now uses system time, so likely different from dt_with_now
    assert dt_with_now != dt_without_now or dt_without_now is None or dt_with_now is None

import pytest
from app.ai.personas import get_conversation_style

def test_get_conversation_style_returns_dict_with_required_keys():
    style_config = get_conversation_style("friendly_viewing")
    assert isinstance(style_config, dict)
    assert "escalation_behavior" in style_config
    assert "phone_fetching_type" in style_config
    assert "strategy" in style_config
    assert "conversation_goal" in style_config

def test_get_conversation_style_returns_consistent_values_for_known_style():
    style_config = get_conversation_style("friendly_viewing")
    # Assert specific keys have expected types or values (example: strings or non-empty)
    assert isinstance(style_config["escalation_behavior"], str)
    assert isinstance(style_config["phone_fetching_type"], str)
    assert isinstance(style_config["strategy"], str)
    assert isinstance(style_config["conversation_goal"], str)
    # Values should not be empty strings
    assert style_config["escalation_behavior"] != ""
    assert style_config["phone_fetching_type"] != ""
    assert style_config["strategy"] != ""
    assert style_config["conversation_goal"] != ""

def test_get_conversation_style_exact_match_required():
    style_config_1 = get_conversation_style("friendly_viewing")
    # Different string that is a substring or variant should not match the same config
    style_config_2 = get_conversation_style("friendly")
    style_config_3 = get_conversation_style("Friendly_Viewing")
    # Because normalization is done outside, these should not return the same dict as exact "friendly_viewing"
    assert style_config_2 != style_config_1
    assert style_config_3 != style_config_1

def test_get_conversation_style_returns_dict_for_another_known_style_if_exists():
    # Since only "friendly_viewing" is known from spec, test that another style returns a dict with required keys
    # We assume "formal_inquiry" might be a style key for test purposes
    style = "formal_inquiry"
    style_config = get_conversation_style(style)
    assert isinstance(style_config, dict)
    assert "escalation_behavior" in style_config
    assert "phone_fetching_type" in style_config
    assert "strategy" in style_config
    assert "conversation_goal" in style_config

def test_get_conversation_style_returns_dict_for_unrecognized_style():
    # Behavior on invalid style is UNSPECIFIED, but function returns a dict with required keys for known styles only.
    # We test that an unknown style still returns a dict with required keys (fallback or default)
    unknown_style = "non_existent_style"
    style_config = get_conversation_style(unknown_style)
    assert isinstance(style_config, dict)
    assert "escalation_behavior" in style_config
    assert "phone_fetching_type" in style_config
    assert "strategy" in style_config
    assert "conversation_goal" in style_config

import pytest
from datetime import datetime, timedelta
from app.ai.stages import extract_viewing_datetime

def test_returns_none_for_empty_or_none_messages():
    assert extract_viewing_datetime([]) is None
    assert extract_viewing_datetime(None) is None

def test_skips_messages_without_text_key_or_non_string_text():
    messages = [
        {'no_text': 'viewing was yesterday'},
        {'text': 12345},
        {'text': 'Viewing happened today at 3pm'}
    ]
    # Assuming the last message contains a valid datetime, function should extract from it
    result = extract_viewing_datetime(messages, now=datetime(2024, 6, 1, 12, 0))
    assert isinstance(result, datetime)

def test_case_insensitive_keyword_matching_and_whole_word():
    messages = [
        {'text': 'I booked a viewing yesterday.'},
        {'text': 'The VIEWING was last week.'},
        {'text': 'viewingly this is not a keyword.'},
        {'text': 'PreViewing is not a match.'}
    ]
    now = datetime(2024, 6, 10, 12, 0)
    result = extract_viewing_datetime(messages, now=now)
    assert isinstance(result, datetime)
    # The first message with a valid keyword is the first one, so result should correspond to that
    # We cannot assert exact datetime due to unspecified patterns, but we can assert ordering by messages
    # So the result should not be None
    assert result is not None

def test_returns_none_if_no_valid_viewing_datetime_found():
    messages = [
        {'text': 'No relevant info here.'},
        {'text': 'Nothing about viewing or dates.'}
    ]
    assert extract_viewing_datetime(messages) is None

def test_processes_messages_in_order_and_returns_first_valid_datetime():
    now = datetime(2024, 6, 15, 12, 0)
    messages = [
        {'text': 'Viewing booked for tomorrow.'},  # first valid
        {'text': 'Viewing was last week.'},        # second valid but should be ignored
    ]
    result = extract_viewing_datetime(messages, now=now)
    assert isinstance(result, datetime)
    # The result should correspond to the first message's datetime, so it should be after now
    assert result > now

def test_now_parameter_affects_relative_date_extraction():
    messages = [
        {'text': 'Viewing happened yesterday.'}
    ]
    now = datetime(2024, 6, 20, 12, 0)
    result = extract_viewing_datetime(messages, now=now)
    assert isinstance(result, datetime)
    # The extracted datetime should be one day before now
    expected = now - timedelta(days=1)
    # Allow some tolerance for time component if any, but exact match is expected per spec
    assert result.date() == expected.date()

from app.ai.stages import detect_stage
import pytest

def test_returns_none_for_empty_list():
    assert detect_stage([]) is None

@pytest.mark.parametrize("messages,expected", [
    ([{"text": "Deploying to production environment"}], "prod"),
    ([{"text": "QA tests passed"}], "test"),
    ([{"text": "Setup for staging server"}], "stage"),
    ([{"text": "Development build ready"}], "dev"),
])
def test_returns_canonical_stage_for_aliases(messages, expected):
    assert detect_stage(messages) == expected

def test_case_insensitive_and_whole_word_matching():
    # "PROD" uppercase, "qa" lowercase, "Devel" mixed case
    assert detect_stage([{"text": "Ready for PROD"}]) == "prod"
    assert detect_stage([{"text": "Passed all qa checks"}]) == "test"
    assert detect_stage([{"text": "Started Devel cycle"}]) == "dev"
    # "prod" should not match "productionX" (not whole word)
    assert detect_stage([{"text": "productionX environment"}]) is None
    # "prod" should match "prod" exactly
    assert detect_stage([{"text": "prod environment"}]) == "prod"

def test_first_matching_keyword_determines_stage():
    messages = [
        {"text": "This is a dev environment"},
        {"text": "Now running tests"},
        {"text": "Production ready"},
    ]
    # "dev" in first message should determine stage
    assert detect_stage(messages) == "dev"

def test_ignores_empty_and_whitespace_only_texts():
    messages = [
        {"text": "   "},
        {"text": ""},
        {"text": "\n\t"},
        {"text": "QA environment is stable"},
    ]
    assert detect_stage(messages) == "test"

def test_returns_none_if_no_keywords_found():
    messages = [
        {"text": "No relevant keywords here"},
        {"text": "Still nothing important"},
    ]
    assert detect_stage(messages) is None

import pytest
from app.ai.conversation_memory import landlord_messages

class Message:
    """Mock message object with dict-like behavior and _content method."""
    def __init__(self, content, sender=None):
        self._content_text = content
        self.sender = sender
    def __getitem__(self, key):
        # simulate dict-like access, but landlord_messages does not specify keys used
        return getattr(self, key, None)
    def _content(self):
        return self._content_text

def _content(message):
    # helper to simulate the _content function used by landlord_messages
    return message._content()

def test_landlord_messages_none_input_returns_empty_list():
    result = landlord_messages(None)
    assert result == []

def test_landlord_messages_empty_list_returns_empty_list():
    result = landlord_messages([])
    assert result == []

def test_landlord_messages_returns_only_landlord_messages_in_order():
    # We do not know exact criteria, but simulate landlord messages by content or sender
    # We assume landlord_messages uses _content(message) internally.
    # We create messages with content indicating landlord or not, 
    # but since criteria is UNSPECIFIED, we simulate landlord messages as those with content starting with "Landlord:"
    # We expect landlord_messages to filter only those.
    messages = [
        Message("Tenant: Hello", sender="tenant"),
        Message("Landlord: Please provide your phone number", sender="landlord"),
        Message("Tenant: Sure, here it is", sender="tenant"),
        Message("Landlord: Thank you", sender="landlord"),
    ]
    filtered = landlord_messages(messages)
    # filtered should contain only the two landlord messages, in order
    assert filtered == [messages[1], messages[3]]

def test_landlord_messages_returns_empty_list_if_no_landlord_messages():
    messages = [
        Message("Tenant: Hello", sender="tenant"),
        Message("Agent: How can I help?", sender="agent"),
        Message("Tenant: I want to book a viewing", sender="tenant"),
    ]
    filtered = landlord_messages(messages)
    assert filtered == []

def test_landlord_messages_preserves_order_for_multiple_landlord_messages():
    messages = [
        Message("Landlord: First message", sender="landlord"),
        Message("Landlord: Second message", sender="landlord"),
        Message("Landlord: Third message", sender="landlord"),
    ]
    filtered = landlord_messages(messages)
    assert filtered == messages
    # Check order preserved: first message first, last message last
    assert filtered[0]._content() == "Landlord: First message"
    assert filtered[-1]._content() == "Landlord: Third message"

def test_landlord_messages_handles_mixed_case_content_and_returns_correct_messages():
    # Since matching criteria is UNSPECIFIED, we do not test case-insensitivity here.
    # But we test that landlord_messages returns only landlord messages regardless of case in content.
    messages = [
        Message("landlord: Please call me", sender="landlord"),
        Message("LANDLORD: Are you available?", sender="landlord"),
        Message("Tenant: Yes, I am", sender="tenant"),
        Message("LaNdLoRd: Let's meet", sender="landlord"),
    ]
    filtered = landlord_messages(messages)
    assert filtered == [messages[0], messages[1], messages[3]]
